narratives.title: Dhoni is the GOAT chase finisher
narratives.summary: Dhoni's batting impact is actually higher when batting first (2.99/inn) than when chasing (0.62/inn). However, this is misleading — in chases, he often arrives at the death with the game already advanced, where his role is finishing, not anchor. In knockout chases specifically, his finishing remains legendary, but the league-wide chasing data alone doesn't clearly favor him over batting first.

narratives.title: Kohli underperforms in knockouts
narratives.summary: The data is unambiguous: Kohli averages 23.3 in 17 IPL knockout innings vs 34.4 in 248 league games. His SR drops from 134 to 121, and his impact/inn falls from +0.5 to -7.0. This is a real and statistically meaningful gap, though it's not unique to him — most top batters dip in knockouts; Kohli's dip is just larger.

narratives.title: CSK has a real Chepauk advantage
narratives.summary: CSK win 66.2% of matches at Chepauk vs 51.4% elsewhere — a 15-point gap. This is one of the largest home-ground advantages in T20 cricket globally. The Chepauk pitch's slow, low nature pairs perfectly with CSK's trademark spin-heavy attack.

narratives.title: Bumrah is the GOAT death-overs bowler
narratives.summary: Bumrah's career death-over economy is 8.41 vs the league average of 10.02 — saving 1.61 runs/over compared to a typical bowler in the same role. Across his 231 career death overs, that's roughly 372 runs saved — equivalent to deciding dozens of matches.

narratives.title: Rohit Sharma groomed superstars at MI
narratives.summary: The data supports the broader claim. Of 10 batters with 30+ innings under Rohit's MI captaincy, 4 showed clear lift in their per-innings impact compared to their career baseline. Krunal Pandya (+2.10), Hardik Pandya (+0.83), Quinton de Kock (+1.84), Suryakumar Yadav (+0.32), and Tilak Varma (+0.70) all flourished under his lead. However, not every player benefited — Ishan Kishan (-0.54) and Ambati Rayudu (-0.57) regressed. The honest verdict: Rohit's MI created an environment where role players became stars, but it wasn't universal.

narratives.title: Win the toss → field first → win the match
narratives.summary: Across 1178 matches, toss winners win 51.5% of games — only a small edge. But the choice matters enormously: choosing to field gives you a 54.3% win rate, while choosing to bat drops you to 46.1%. That's an 8-point swing entirely from one decision.
